title: Standing Up For You | Helen Whately

text_excerpt: Standing Up For You | Helen Whately Skip to main content Main navigation Home News About Helen Newsletter My Articles Listening to You Standing Up For You Tweet Some of the most impactful work I do as an MP is helping people pull together to make things happen in their community. I work with public bodies, local charities and community groups – and often just determined individuals – to support the efforts people are making to improve life where they live. My job is to be your voice in Parliament and stand up for you when things aren’t working as they should. Whether it is changing unfair ‘fleecehold’ laws, holding South East Water to account for their failures, or taking up your personal cases with government departments and public bodies, I will always stand up for the people of Faversham and Mid Kent. News We must never suffer water outages like the ones earlier this year again Monday, 13 April, 2026 South East Water is due to be fined £22million by regulator OfWat. 'Fleecehold' has got to stop. Wednesday, 18 March, 2026 'Fleecehold' (where management companies charge egregious fees on new housing estates) has got to stop.That’s why I was pleased to support the Home Owners Rights Network meeting (known as Hornets) earlier this month in Parliament.I am also meeting residents’ groups paying ‘fleecehold’ from across th Fighting for accountability from South East Water Wednesday, 4 February, 2026 Fighting for accountability from South East WaterSouth East Water’s response to the recent water outages was abysmal. Thousands of people were without water for days, forced to buy water from supermarkets or drive a two hour round trip all the way to Headcorn. Fleecehold Update Wednesday, 4 February, 2026 Finally, the ending of ‘fleecehold’ is in sightThere is an urgent need to protect freeholders and leaseholders against ‘fleeceholder’ fees.The Government has finally opened its consultation on ‘enhanced protections for homeowners on freehold estates’ which closes on 12 March 2026. Meet your MP Cafe Surgeries: Harrietsham, Hollingbourne & Bearsted Thursday, 20 November, 2025 Following the success of several ‘meet and greets’ in pubs, cafes and village halls in the new parts of my constituency, I am now hosting events across the whole of Faversham and Mid Kent. My latest gatherings were in Harrietsham, Hollingbourne and Bearsted. End Fleecehold Now Monday, 17 November, 2025 We need an urgent legal change to protect freeholders and leaseholders against ‘fleeceholder’ fees.The last Government passed the Leasehold and Freehold Reform Act which just needs some simple secondary legislation to put it into practice - but this Government keeps dragging its feet. They are Continuing my ‘Meet your MP' sessions in Leeds Village Wednesday, 27 August, 2025 Following the success of several ‘meet and greets’ in pubs, cafes and village halls in the new parts of my constituency, I am now hosting events across the whole of Faversham and Mid Kent. My latest gathering was at Ledian Gardens Retirement Community in Leeds Village. Water shortages Thursday, 7 August, 2025 Photo caption: Speaking to BBC South East Today about the unacceptable length of time that some local residents have been left without running water In July hundreds of local households, businesses and farmers in East Kent had no water supply for several days. Continuing the fight to crack-down on ‘fleecehold’ estate management companies Tuesday, 24 June, 2025 Photo caption: Meeting Halima Ali, the Chairwoman of Homeowners Rights Network (HorNets) when she came to Parliament to talk to the select committee examining regulation of property managers. Southern Water finally admit Teynham's sewer network is in poor condition Tuesday, 25 March, 2025 Since Teynham joined my constituency at the General Election last year it was brought to my attention that sewage regularly floods in Frognal Lane and Frognal Gardens.
